---
title: "Nook and Care – System Design Documentation"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
    fig-width: 160
    fig-height: 120
    include-after-body:
      text: |
        <script>
        (function() {
          function addFullscreenButtons() {
            // Strategy: Find figure elements that contain SVGs (Quarto wraps mermaid in <figure>)
            var figures = document.querySelectorAll('figure');
            var processed = 0;
            
            figures.forEach(function(figure) {
              // Skip if already processed
              if (figure.dataset.fullscreenAdded === 'true') return;
              
              // Find SVG inside this figure
              var svg = figure.querySelector('svg');
              if (!svg) return;
              
              // Check if it's a diagram (has viewBox and substantial content)
              var viewBox = svg.getAttribute('viewBox');
              var innerHTML = svg.innerHTML || '';
              
              // Must have viewBox and substantial content (diagrams, not icons)
              if (!viewBox || innerHTML.length < 500) return;
              
              figure.dataset.fullscreenAdded = 'true';
              processed++;
              
              // Make figure container relative for button positioning
              var currentStyle = figure.style.cssText || '';
              figure.style.cssText = currentStyle + 'position: relative;';
              
              // Create fullscreen button
              var btn = document.createElement('button');
              btn.className = 'mermaid-fullscreen-btn';
              btn.innerHTML = '🔍 Fullscreen';
              btn.type = 'button';
              btn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Open diagram in fullscreen');
              figure.appendChild(btn);
              
              // Create modal overlay (one per figure)
              var modal = document.createElement('div');
              modal.className = 'mermaid-modal';
              
              var modalContent = document.createElement('div');
              modalContent.className = 'mermaid-modal-content';
              
              var closeBtn = document.createElement('button');
              closeBtn.className = 'mermaid-modal-close';
              closeBtn.innerHTML = '&times;';
              closeBtn.type = 'button';
              closeBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', 'Close');
              
              var diagramArea = document.createElement('div');
              diagramArea.className = 'mermaid-modal-diagram';
              
              modalContent.appendChild(closeBtn);
              modalContent.appendChild(diagramArea);
              modal.appendChild(modalContent);
              document.body.appendChild(modal);
              
              // Open modal: clone the SVG fresh each time
              btn.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                e.preventDefault();
                e.stopPropagation();
                
                // Clone the SVG
                var cloned = svg.cloneNode(true);
                
                // Preserve viewBox, remove fixed width/height so it scales
                var vb = svg.getAttribute('viewBox');
                if (vb) {
                  cloned.setAttribute('viewBox', vb);
                  // Allow scaling to fill space
                  cloned.setAttribute('preserveAspectRatio', 'xMidYMid meet');
                }
                cloned.removeAttribute('width');
                cloned.removeAttribute('height');
                // Make SVG fill the modal - calculate available space
                var availableWidth = window.innerWidth * 0.95 - 40; // 95vw minus padding
                var availableHeight = window.innerHeight * 0.95 - 68; // 95vh minus padding
                // Set explicit dimensions to fill space
                cloned.setAttribute('width', availableWidth);
                cloned.setAttribute('height', availableHeight);
                cloned.style.cssText = 'display:block; width:' + availableWidth + 'px; height:' + availableHeight + 'px; max-width:100%; max-height:100%;';
                
                diagramArea.innerHTML = '';
                diagramArea.appendChild(cloned);
                
                modal.style.display = 'flex';
                document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden';
              });
              
              function closeModal() {
                modal.style.display = 'none';
                document.body.style.overflow = '';
              }
              
              closeBtn.addEventListener('click', closeModal);
              modal.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                if (e.target === modal) closeModal();
              });
              document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e) {
                if (e.key === 'Escape' && modal.style.display === 'flex') closeModal();
              });
            });
            
            // Fallback: If no figures found, try direct SVG approach
            if (processed === 0) {
              var allSvgs = document.querySelectorAll('svg');
              allSvgs.forEach(function(svg) {
                if (svg.dataset.fullscreenAdded === 'true') return;
                
                var viewBox = svg.getAttribute('viewBox');
                var innerHTML = svg.innerHTML || '';
                
                // Must be a substantial diagram
                if (!viewBox || innerHTML.length < 500) return;
                
                svg.dataset.fullscreenAdded = 'true';
                
                // Wrap SVG in container
                var parent = svg.parentElement;
                var container = document.createElement('div');
                container.className = 'mermaid-container';
                container.style.cssText = 'position: relative; display: block; width: 100%;';
                parent.insertBefore(container, svg);
                container.appendChild(svg);
                
                // Create button and modal (same as above)
                var btn = document.createElement('button');
                btn.className = 'mermaid-fullscreen-btn';
                btn.innerHTML = '🔍 Fullscreen';
                btn.type = 'button';
                container.appendChild(btn);
                
                var modal = document.createElement('div');
                modal.className = 'mermaid-modal';
                var modalContent = document.createElement('div');
                modalContent.className = 'mermaid-modal-content';
                var closeBtn = document.createElement('button');
                closeBtn.className = 'mermaid-modal-close';
                closeBtn.innerHTML = '&times;';
                closeBtn.type = 'button';
                var diagramArea = document.createElement('div');
                diagramArea.className = 'mermaid-modal-diagram';
                modalContent.appendChild(closeBtn);
                modalContent.appendChild(diagramArea);
                modal.appendChild(modalContent);
                document.body.appendChild(modal);
                
                btn.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                  e.preventDefault();
                  e.stopPropagation();
                  var cloned = svg.cloneNode(true);
                  var vb = svg.getAttribute('viewBox');
                  if (vb) {
                    cloned.setAttribute('viewBox', vb);
                    cloned.setAttribute('preserveAspectRatio', 'xMidYMid meet');
                  }
                  cloned.removeAttribute('width');
                  cloned.removeAttribute('height');
                  cloned.style.cssText = 'display:block; width:100%; height:auto; min-height:80vh; max-width:100%;';
                  diagramArea.innerHTML = '';
                  diagramArea.appendChild(cloned);
                  modal.style.display = 'flex';
                  document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden';
                });
                
                function closeModal() {
                  modal.style.display = 'none';
                  document.body.style.overflow = '';
                }
                closeBtn.addEventListener('click', closeModal);
                modal.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
                  if (e.target === modal) closeModal();
                });
                document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e) {
                  if (e.key === 'Escape' && modal.style.display === 'flex') closeModal();
                });
              });
            }
          }
          
          // Run immediately and also after delays to catch late-rendered content
          if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
            document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', addFullscreenButtons);
          } else {
            addFullscreenButtons();
          }
          
          // Retry after delays
          setTimeout(addFullscreenButtons, 500);
          setTimeout(addFullscreenButtons, 1500);
          setTimeout(addFullscreenButtons, 3000);
        })();
        </script>
    css: mermaid-fullscreen.css
---

# Project Overview

This document describes the architecture for **Nook and Care**, a senior housing marketplace platform designed to help families find verified senior housing facilities.

The goal of the system is to help:

- **Families and Seniors** search for and discover verified senior housing facilities
- **Housing Providers** list and manage their senior care facilities
- **Platform Administrators** verify listings and manage the marketplace

The system allows users to:

1. Search and discover senior housing listings
2. View detailed facility listings with images, amenities, and care types
3. Book tours (in-person or virtual) for facilities
4. Compare listings side-by-side
5. Leave reviews and ratings
6. Manage subscriptions and payments (for providers)
7. Report fraud and verify providers

The design is built as a **marketplace platform** supporting many providers and families using the same shared marketplace.

---

# User Stories

## Provider (Housing Facility Owner)

As a **provider**, I want to:

- Create and manage **senior housing listings**
- Upload **facility images**
- Configure **amenities, care types, and room types** for my facilities
- Set **pricing** for different room types
- Manage **tour bookings** and availability
- Receive and respond to **tour requests**
- View **reviews and ratings** from families
- Manage **subscriptions** (Free, Pro, Premium plans)
- Upgrade listings to **featured** status

## Family / Senior (Registered User)

As a **family member or senior**, I want to:

- Search and filter senior housing by location, care type, budget, and room type
- View detailed facility information with images and amenities
- Compare multiple listings side-by-side
- Book **tours** (in-person or virtual) for facilities
- Save **favorite listings** for later
- Track my **tour bookings**
- Leave **reviews and ratings** after visits
- Report **fraud or fake listings**

## Platform Administrator

As an **administrator**, I want to:

- Verify **providers** through business verification workflow
- Approve or reject **listings** before publication
- Review and moderate **reports** (fraud, fake listings, misleading images)
- Manage **subscription plans** and payments
- Monitor platform analytics and performance
- Handle **verification** requests (government license checks, AI image validation)


# Industry Workflow

Typical workflow for senior housing marketplace:

1. **Provider Registration**: Housing provider creates account and completes business verification
2. **Provider Subscription**: Provider subscribes to a plan (Free, Pro, or Premium)
3. **Listing Creation**: Provider adds facility details, images, amenities, care types, and room types
4. **Admin Verification**: Admin verifies provider credentials and approves listing
5. **Family Search**: Family searches for senior housing matching their criteria (location, care type, budget, room type)
6. **Listing Comparison**: Family compares multiple listings side-by-side
7. **Tour Booking**: Family selects listing, chooses date, and sends tour booking request
8. **Provider Approval**: Provider approves tour request
9. **Tour Confirmation**: System sends confirmation to family
10. **Property Visit**: Family visits facility for tour
11. **Placement**: Family decides on placement (outside system scope)
12. **Review Process**: Family leaves review and rating after visit
13. **Provider Response**: Provider can respond to reviews

---

# System Architecture

The system is designed as a **marketplace platform**.

Key components:

- Users (Families/Seniors, Providers, Admin)
- Senior Housing Listings
- Tour Bookings
- Reviews and Ratings
- Payments and Subscriptions
- Provider Verification
- Fraud Reporting



---

# Entity Relationship Diagram

This is the **simple relationship view** of the system.

> **Tip:** Click the **🔍 Fullscreen** button on each diagram to view it in fullscreen mode for better visibility.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme':'base', 'themeVariables': {'fontSize':'48px', 'fontFamily':'Arial, sans-serif', 'primaryColor':'#ff6b6b', 'primaryTextColor':'#fff', 'primaryBorderColor':'#c92a2a', 'lineColor':'#495057', 'secondaryColor':'#4dabf7', 'tertiaryColor':'#51cf66', 'tertiaryTextColor':'#000', 'noteTextColor':'#000', 'noteBkgColor':'#fff', 'noteBorderColor':'#000', 'textColor':'#000'}, 'flowchart':{'nodeSpacing':60, 'rankSpacing':90}, 'er':{'entityPadding':18, 'fill':'#fff', 'stroke':'#495057', 'strokeWidth':2.5, 'fontSize':'48px'}}}%%
erDiagram

USERS ||--o{ PROVIDERS : can_be
USERS ||--o{ TOURS : books
USERS ||--o{ REVIEWS : writes
USERS ||--o{ FAVORITES : has

PROVIDERS ||--o{ LISTINGS : owns
PROVIDERS ||--o{ SUBSCRIPTIONS : has

LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_IMAGES : has
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_AMENITIES : has
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_SERVICES : has
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_EQUIPMENT : has
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_LANGUAGES : supports
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_CERTIFICATIONS : has
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_ACTIVITIES : offers
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_DINING_OPTIONS : provides
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_SAFETY_FEATURES : has
LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_INSURANCE_OPTIONS : accepts
LISTINGS ||--o{ HOUSE_RULES : has
LISTINGS ||--o{ TOURS : offers
LISTINGS ||--o{ BOOKINGS : receives
LISTINGS ||--o{ REVIEWS : receives
LISTINGS ||--o{ FAVORITES : in

AMENITIES ||--o{ LISTING_AMENITIES : used_in
TREATMENTS_SERVICES ||--o{ LISTING_SERVICES : used_in
EQUIPMENT ||--o{ LISTING_EQUIPMENT : used_in
LANGUAGES ||--o{ LISTING_LANGUAGES : used_in
CERTIFICATIONS ||--o{ LISTING_CERTIFICATIONS : used_in
ACTIVITIES ||--o{ LISTING_ACTIVITIES : used_in
DINING_OPTIONS ||--o{ LISTING_DINING_OPTIONS : used_in
SAFETY_FEATURES ||--o{ LISTING_SAFETY_FEATURES : used_in
INSURANCE_OPTIONS ||--o{ LISTING_INSURANCE_OPTIONS : used_in

BOOKINGS ||--o{ PAYMENTS : requires
BOOKINGS ||--o{ REVIEWS : generates

USERS ||--o{ VERIFICATIONS : has
USERS ||--o{ REPORTS : files
```

---

# Detailed ER Diagram With Fields

This is the **detailed field-level ER diagram** you can use when drawing the system in Draw.io.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme':'base', 'themeVariables': {'fontSize':'48px', 'fontFamily':'Arial, sans-serif', 'primaryColor':'#ff6b6b', 'primaryTextColor':'#fff', 'primaryBorderColor':'#c92a2a', 'lineColor':'#495057', 'secondaryColor':'#4dabf7', 'tertiaryColor':'#51cf66', 'tertiaryTextColor':'#000', 'noteTextColor':'#000', 'noteBkgColor':'#fff', 'noteBorderColor':'#000', 'textColor':'#000'}, 'flowchart':{'nodeSpacing':60, 'rankSpacing':90}, 'er':{'entityPadding':18, 'fill':'#fff', 'stroke':'#495057', 'strokeWidth':2.5, 'fontSize':'48px'}}}%%
erDiagram
  USERS ||--o{ PROVIDERS : can_be
  USERS ||--o{ TOURS : books
  USERS ||--o{ REVIEWS : writes
  USERS ||--o{ FAVORITES : has
  PROVIDERS ||--o{ LISTINGS : owns
  PROVIDERS ||--o{ SUBSCRIPTIONS : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_IMAGES : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_AMENITIES : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_SERVICES : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_EQUIPMENT : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_LANGUAGES : supports
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_CERTIFICATIONS : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_ACTIVITIES : offers
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_DINING_OPTIONS : provides
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_SAFETY_FEATURES : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ LISTING_INSURANCE_OPTIONS : accepts
  LISTINGS ||--o{ HOUSE_RULES : has
  LISTINGS ||--o{ TOURS : offers
  LISTINGS ||--o{ BOOKINGS : receives
  LISTINGS ||--o{ REVIEWS : receives
  LISTINGS ||--o{ FAVORITES : in
  AMENITIES ||--o{ LISTING_AMENITIES : used_in
  TREATMENTS_SERVICES ||--o{ LISTING_SERVICES : used_in
  EQUIPMENT ||--o{ LISTING_EQUIPMENT : used_in
  LANGUAGES ||--o{ LISTING_LANGUAGES : used_in
  CERTIFICATIONS ||--o{ LISTING_CERTIFICATIONS : used_in
  ACTIVITIES ||--o{ LISTING_ACTIVITIES : used_in
  DINING_OPTIONS ||--o{ LISTING_DINING_OPTIONS : used_in
  SAFETY_FEATURES ||--o{ LISTING_SAFETY_FEATURES : used_in
  INSURANCE_OPTIONS ||--o{ LISTING_INSURANCE_OPTIONS : used_in
  BOOKINGS ||--o{ PAYMENTS : requires
  BOOKINGS ||--o{ REVIEWS : generates
  USERS ||--o{ VERIFICATIONS : has
  USERS ||--o{ REPORTS : files

  USERS {
    uuid id PK
    string email
    string password_hash
    string full_name
    string phone
    string role
    boolean is_active
    datetime email_verified_at
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }

  PROVIDERS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid user_id FK
    string business_name
    string business_type
    string tax_id
    string address
    string city
    string country
    string verification_status
    datetime verified_at
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }

  LISTINGS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid provider_id FK
    string title
    string description
    string care_type
    string room_type
    string address
    string city
    string state
    string country
    string postal_code
    decimal latitude
    decimal longitude
    decimal price
    string currency
    integer capacity
    integer available_beds
    string staff_ratio
    integer established_year
    string phone
    string email
    string license_number
    boolean is_featured
    boolean has_24_hour_care
    string status
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }

  LISTING_IMAGES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    string image_url
    integer display_order
    boolean is_primary
    datetime created_at
  }

  AMENITIES {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string category
    string icon
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_AMENITIES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid amenity_id FK
    datetime created_at
  }

  TREATMENTS_SERVICES {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string description
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_SERVICES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid treatment_service_id FK
    decimal price
    boolean is_included
    datetime created_at
  }

  EQUIPMENT {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string category
    string description
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_EQUIPMENT {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid equipment_id FK
    integer quantity
    datetime created_at
  }

  LANGUAGES {
    uuid id PK
    string code
    string name
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_LANGUAGES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid language_id FK
    datetime created_at
  }

  CERTIFICATIONS {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string description
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_CERTIFICATIONS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid certification_id FK
    string license_number
    datetime created_at
  }

  ACTIVITIES {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string category
    string description
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_ACTIVITIES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid activity_id FK
    datetime created_at
  }

  DINING_OPTIONS {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string description
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_DINING_OPTIONS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid dining_option_id FK
    datetime created_at
  }

  SAFETY_FEATURES {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string category
    string description
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_SAFETY_FEATURES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid safety_feature_id FK
    datetime created_at
  }

  INSURANCE_OPTIONS {
    uuid id PK
    string name
    string description
    datetime created_at
  }

  LISTING_INSURANCE_OPTIONS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid insurance_option_id FK
    datetime created_at
  }

  HOUSE_RULES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    string rule_text
    integer display_order
    datetime created_at
  }

  TOURS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    string tour_type
    datetime scheduled_at
    string status
    uuid booked_by_user_id FK
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }

  BOOKINGS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid user_id FK
    date tour_date
    string tour_type
    string status
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }


  REVIEWS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid booking_id FK
    uuid listing_id FK
    uuid user_id FK
    integer rating
    string comment
    string response_from_provider
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }

  FAVORITES {
    uuid id PK
    uuid user_id FK
    uuid listing_id FK
    datetime created_at
  }

  SUBSCRIPTIONS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid provider_id FK
    string plan_type
    decimal price
    string billing_cycle
    date start_date
    date end_date
    string status
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }

  PAYMENTS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid booking_id FK
    decimal amount
    string currency
    string payment_method
    string transaction_id
    string status
    datetime processed_at
    datetime created_at
  }

  VERIFICATIONS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid user_id FK
    string verification_type
    string document_url
    string status
    datetime verified_at
    uuid verified_by_admin_id FK
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }

  REPORTS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid reported_by_user_id FK
    uuid reported_user_id FK
    uuid listing_id FK
    string report_type
    string description
    string status
    datetime created_at
    datetime updated_at
  }
```

---

# Table Descriptions

## 1) users

**Purpose:** Core user accounts for families, seniors, providers, and administrators.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `email` (VARCHAR(255), UNIQUE)
- `password_hash` (TEXT)
- `full_name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `phone` (VARCHAR(30), NULL)
- `role` (VARCHAR(20)) — FAMILY | SENIOR | PROVIDER | ADMIN
- `is_active` (BOOLEAN)
- `email_verified_at` (TIMESTAMP, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `users 1—* providers` — A user can become a provider
- `users 1—* tours` — Users book tours
- `users 1—* reviews` — Users write reviews
- `users 1—* favorites` — Users save favorite listings
- `users 1—* verifications` — Users can be verified
- `users 1—* reports` — Users can file reports

This table is the foundation for authentication and user management.

---

## 2) providers

**Purpose:** Senior housing facility owners who list their care facilities.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id)
- `business_name` (VARCHAR(150))
- `business_type` (VARCHAR(50))
- `tax_id` (VARCHAR(50), NULL)
- `address` (TEXT)
- `city` (VARCHAR(100))
- `country` (VARCHAR(100))
- `verification_status` (VARCHAR(30)) — PENDING | VERIFIED | REJECTED
- `verified_at` (TIMESTAMP, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `users 1—1 providers` — One user account can have one provider profile
- `providers 1—* listings` — Providers own multiple facility listings
- `providers 1—* subscriptions` — Providers can have subscription plans (Free, Pro, Premium)

This table stores business information for senior housing providers. Providers must complete verification before publishing listings.

---

## 3) listings

**Purpose:** Senior housing facilities available for tours and placement.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `provider_id` (UUID, FK → providers.id)
- `title` (VARCHAR(200))
- `description` (TEXT)
- `care_type` (VARCHAR(50)) — ASSISTED_LIVING | MEMORY_CARE | INDEPENDENT_LIVING | ADULT_FAMILY_HOME | SKILLED_NURSING
- `capacity` (INTEGER) — Total number of residents the facility can accommodate
- `available_beds` (INTEGER) — Number of available beds
- `staff_ratio` (VARCHAR(50), NULL) — Staff to resident ratio (e.g., "1:3")
- `established_year` (INTEGER, NULL) — Year facility was established
- `phone` (VARCHAR(30), NULL) — Contact phone number
- `email` (VARCHAR(255), NULL) — Contact email address
- `license_number` (VARCHAR(100), NULL) — State license number (if applicable)
- `has_24_hour_care` (BOOLEAN) — Whether 24-hour care is available
- `room_type` (VARCHAR(50)) — PRIVATE | SEMI_PRIVATE | SHARED
- `address` (TEXT)
- `city` (VARCHAR(100))
- `state` (VARCHAR(100), NULL)
- `country` (VARCHAR(100))
- `postal_code` (VARCHAR(20), NULL)
- `latitude` (DECIMAL(10,8))
- `longitude` (DECIMAL(11,8))
- `price` (DECIMAL(10,2)) — Monthly or per room pricing
- `currency` (VARCHAR(10))
- `is_featured` (BOOLEAN) — Featured listings for homepage visibility
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — ACTIVE | INACTIVE | PENDING | SUSPENDED
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `providers 1—* listings` — One provider can have many facilities
- `listings 1—* listing_images` — Each listing has multiple images
- `listings 1—* listing_amenities` — Listings have amenities
- `listings 1—* listing_services` — Listings offer care services
- `listings 1—* listing_equipment` — Listings have medical/assistive equipment
- `listings 1—* listing_languages` — Listings support languages
- `listings 1—* listing_certifications` — Listings have certifications/licenses
- `listings 1—* listing_activities` — Listings offer activities and programs
- `listings 1—* listing_dining_options` — Listings provide dining options
- `listings 1—* listing_safety_features` — Listings have safety features
- `listings 1—* listing_insurance_options` — Listings accept insurance/payment types
- `listings 1—* tours` — Listings offer tours (in-person or virtual)
- `listings 1—* reviews` — Listings receive reviews
- `listings 1—* favorites` — Listings can be favorited
- `listings 1—* reports` — Listings can be reported for fraud

This is the core entity representing available senior housing facilities. Listings must be verified before publication.

---

## 4) listing_images

**Purpose:** Images associated with senior housing facility listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `image_url` (TEXT)
- `display_order` (INTEGER)
- `is_primary` (BOOLEAN)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings 1—* listing_images` — Each listing has multiple images
- One image should be marked as primary for display purposes
- Display order allows providers to organize images
- Images are validated by AI for fraud prevention

---

## 5) amenities

**Purpose:** Master catalog of available amenities (pool, WiFi, parking, etc.).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `category` (VARCHAR(50)) — BASIC | PREMIUM | SAFETY | ACCESSIBILITY
- `icon` (VARCHAR(100), NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `amenities 1—* listing_amenities` — Amenities are used across many listings
- This is a master catalog to ensure consistency

---

## 6) listing_amenities

**Purpose:** Junction table linking listings to their amenities.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `amenity_id` (UUID, FK → amenities.id)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* amenities` — Many-to-many relationship
- A listing can have many amenities, and an amenity can be in many listings

**Suggested constraint:**

- Unique (`listing_id`, `amenity_id`) to prevent duplicates

---

## 7) treatments_services

**Purpose:** Master catalog of treatments and services that can be offered (medication management, diabetes management, etc.).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `description` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `treatments_services 1—* listing_services` — Treatments/services are offered by multiple listings
- Master catalog ensures consistency

---

## 8) listing_services

**Purpose:** Treatments and services offered by specific listings with custom pricing.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `treatment_service_id` (UUID, FK → treatments_services.id)
- `price` (DECIMAL(10,2))
- `is_included` (BOOLEAN) — Whether service is included in base price
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* treatments_services` — Many-to-many with pricing
- Allows each listing to set custom prices for treatments/services

---

## 9) equipment

**Purpose:** Master catalog of equipment available (kitchen appliances, electronics, etc.).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `category` (VARCHAR(50))
- `description` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `equipment 1—* listing_equipment` — Equipment is used across listings
- Master catalog ensures consistency

---

## 10) listing_equipment

**Purpose:** Equipment available at specific listings with quantities.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `equipment_id` (UUID, FK → equipment.id)
- `quantity` (INTEGER)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* equipment` — Many-to-many with quantity tracking
- Tracks how many of each equipment item is available

---

## 11) languages

**Purpose:** Master catalog of languages supported.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `code` (VARCHAR(10)) — ISO language code (en, es, fr, etc.)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `languages 1—* listing_languages` — Languages are used across listings
- Standard language codes for consistency

---

## 12) listing_languages

**Purpose:** Languages supported by specific listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `language_id` (UUID, FK → languages.id)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* languages` — Many-to-many relationship
- Indicates which languages the provider can communicate in

---

## 13) certifications

**Purpose:** Master catalog of certifications and licenses (State licensed, Medicare certified, Medicaid certified, VA approved, Joint Commission accredited).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `description` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `certifications 1—* listing_certifications` — Certifications are used across listings
- Master catalog ensures consistency for filtering

---

## 14) listing_certifications

**Purpose:** Certifications and licenses held by specific listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `certification_id` (UUID, FK → certifications.id)
- `license_number` (VARCHAR(100), NULL) — License number if applicable
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* certifications` — Many-to-many relationship
- Tracks which certifications/licenses each facility holds
- Used for filtering (e.g., "State licensed", "Medicare certified")

---

## 15) activities

**Purpose:** Master catalog of activities and programs offered (Gardening, Game nights, Social activities, etc.).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `category` (VARCHAR(50)) — RECREATIONAL | SOCIAL | FITNESS | EDUCATIONAL
- `description` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `activities 1—* listing_activities` — Activities are offered by multiple listings
- Master catalog ensures consistency

---

## 16) listing_activities

**Purpose:** Activities and programs offered by specific listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `activity_id` (UUID, FK → activities.id)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* activities` — Many-to-many relationship
- Tracks activities and programs available at each facility

---

## 17) dining_options

**Purpose:** Master catalog of dining options (Three home-cooked meals daily, Special dietary accommodations, Family-style dining, etc.).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `description` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `dining_options 1—* listing_dining_options` — Dining options are used across listings
- Master catalog ensures consistency

---

## 18) listing_dining_options

**Purpose:** Dining options provided by specific listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `dining_option_id` (UUID, FK → dining_options.id)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* dining_options` — Many-to-many relationship
- Tracks dining services and meal options at each facility

---

## 19) safety_features

**Purpose:** Master catalog of safety features (Emergency call system, Fire safety, Fall prevention, Wheelchair accessible, etc.).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `category` (VARCHAR(50)) — EMERGENCY | FIRE | ACCESSIBILITY | MEDICAL
- `description` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `safety_features 1—* listing_safety_features` — Safety features are used across listings
- Master catalog ensures consistency

---

## 20) listing_safety_features

**Purpose:** Safety features available at specific listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `safety_feature_id` (UUID, FK → safety_features.id)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* safety_features` — Many-to-many relationship
- Tracks safety and accessibility features at each facility

---

## 21) insurance_options

**Purpose:** Master catalog of insurance and payment options (Medicare, Private insurance, Long-term care insurance, VA benefits).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `name` (VARCHAR(100))
- `description` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `insurance_options 1—* listing_insurance_options` — Insurance options are used across listings
- Master catalog ensures consistency for filtering

---

## 22) listing_insurance_options

**Purpose:** Insurance and payment options accepted by specific listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `insurance_option_id` (UUID, FK → insurance_options.id)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings *—* insurance_options` — Many-to-many relationship
- Tracks which insurance types and payment methods each facility accepts
- Used for filtering (e.g., "Medicare", "Private insurance", "Long-term care insurance", "VA benefits")

---

## 23) house_rules

**Purpose:** Rules and policies for specific listings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `rule_text` (TEXT)
- `display_order` (INTEGER)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings 1—* house_rules` — Each listing can have multiple rules
- Display order allows providers to prioritize rules

---

## 24) tours

**Purpose:** Virtual or in-person tours scheduled for senior housing facilities.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `tour_type` (VARCHAR(50)) — VIRTUAL | IN_PERSON
- `scheduled_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — PENDING | APPROVED | SCHEDULED | COMPLETED | CANCELLED
- `booked_by_user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings 1—* tours` — Listings can offer multiple tours
- `users 1—* tours` — Users (families/seniors) can book tours
- Workflow: User selects listing → selects date → sends booking request → provider approves → confirmation sent

---

## 25) bookings

**Purpose:** Tour bookings made by families/seniors for facility tours.

**Note:** This table may be merged with `tours` table or kept separate depending on implementation. The SRS mentions tour booking workflow, so this could represent tour bookings rather than accommodation bookings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id)
- `tour_date` (DATE)
- `tour_type` (VARCHAR(50)) — VIRTUAL | IN_PERSON
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — PENDING | APPROVED | CONFIRMED | COMPLETED | CANCELLED
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings 1—* bookings` — Listings receive multiple tour bookings
- `users 1—* bookings` — Users make multiple tour bookings
- `bookings 1—* reviews` — Completed tours can generate reviews

This represents tour bookings for senior housing facilities.

---

## 26) reviews

**Purpose:** Reviews and ratings left by families/seniors after facility tours or placement.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `booking_id` (UUID, FK → bookings.id, NULL) — Optional, if review is after tour
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id)
- `rating` (INTEGER) — 1 to 5
- `comment` (TEXT, NULL)
- `response_from_provider` (TEXT, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `listings 1—* reviews` — Listings accumulate reviews
- `users 1—* reviews` — Users write multiple reviews
- Providers can respond to reviews
- Admin moderates reviews

---

## 27) favorites

**Purpose:** Saved listings that users want to revisit.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id)
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `users 1—* favorites` — Users can favorite multiple listings
- `listings 1—* favorites` — Listings can be favorited by multiple users
- Many-to-many relationship

**Suggested constraint:**

- Unique (`user_id`, `listing_id`) to prevent duplicate favorites

---

## 28) subscriptions

**Purpose:** Subscription plans for providers to publish listings. Plans include Free (limited listings), Pro (unlimited listings), and Premium (featured listings).

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `provider_id` (UUID, FK → providers.id)
- `plan_type` (VARCHAR(50)) — FREE | PRO | PREMIUM
- `price` (DECIMAL(10,2))
- `billing_cycle` (VARCHAR(20)) — MONTHLY | YEARLY
- `start_date` (DATE)
- `end_date` (DATE)
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — ACTIVE | EXPIRED | CANCELLED
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `providers 1—* subscriptions` — Providers can have subscription history
- Providers must subscribe to publish listings
- Premium plan enables featured listings with homepage visibility and search ranking boost

---

## 29) payments

**Purpose:** Payment transactions for bookings.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `booking_id` (UUID, FK → bookings.id)
- `amount` (DECIMAL(10,2))
- `currency` (VARCHAR(10))
- `payment_method` (VARCHAR(50)) — CREDIT_CARD | PAYPAL | BANK_TRANSFER
- `transaction_id` (VARCHAR(255), NULL)
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — PENDING | COMPLETED | FAILED | REFUNDED
- `processed_at` (TIMESTAMP, NULL)
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `bookings 1—1 payments` — Each booking has one payment
- Tracks payment processing and status

---

## 30) verifications

**Purpose:** Verification system for providers including government license checks, manual admin review, and AI image validation. Verified providers receive a verification badge.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id) — Typically for providers
- `verification_type` (VARCHAR(50)) — BUSINESS_LICENSE | GOVERNMENT_LICENSE | IDENTITY | IMAGE_VALIDATION
- `document_url` (TEXT, NULL)
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — PENDING | VERIFIED | REJECTED
- `verified_at` (TIMESTAMP, NULL)
- `verified_by_admin_id` (UUID, FK → users.id, NULL) — Admin who verified
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `users 1—* verifications` — Providers can have multiple verification types
- Ensures platform security and trust
- Verification includes government license checks, manual admin review, and AI image validation

---

## 31) reports

**Purpose:** Reporting system for users to report fake listings, scam providers, or misleading images. Admin receives alerts.

### Fields

- `id` (UUID, PK)
- `reported_by_user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id)
- `reported_user_id` (UUID, FK → users.id, NULL) — Provider being reported
- `listing_id` (UUID, FK → listings.id, NULL) — Listing being reported
- `report_type` (VARCHAR(50)) — FAKE_LISTING | SCAM_PROVIDER | MISLEADING_IMAGES | FRAUD | OTHER
- `description` (TEXT)
- `status` (VARCHAR(30)) — PENDING | REVIEWED | RESOLVED | DISMISSED
- `created_at` (TIMESTAMP)
- `updated_at` (TIMESTAMP)

### Relationships (why)

- `users 1—* reports` — Users can file reports
- `listings 1—* reports` — Listings can be reported
- Enables fraud prevention and platform safety
- Admin receives alerts for review

---

# Relationship Summary

- `users 1—1 providers`
- `users 1—* tours` — Users book tours
- `users 1—* reviews` — Users write reviews
- `users 1—* favorites` — Users save favorite listings
- `users 1—* verifications` — Users can be verified
- `users 1—* reports` (as reporter and reported)
- `providers 1—* listings` — Providers own multiple facilities
- `providers 1—* subscriptions` — Providers have subscription plans
- `listings 1—* listing_images` — Listings have images
- `listings *—* amenities` (through listing_amenities)
- `listings *—* treatments_services` (through listing_services) — Treatments and care services
- `listings *—* equipment` (through listing_equipment) — Medical/assistive equipment
- `listings *—* languages` (through listing_languages)
- `listings *—* certifications` (through listing_certifications) — Certifications and licenses
- `listings *—* activities` (through listing_activities) — Activities and programs
- `listings *—* dining_options` (through listing_dining_options) — Dining services
- `listings *—* safety_features` (through listing_safety_features) — Safety and accessibility features
- `listings *—* insurance_options` (through listing_insurance_options) — Accepted insurance and payment types
- `listings 1—* house_rules` — Facility rules and policies
- `listings 1—* tours` — Listings offer tours
- `listings 1—* reviews` — Listings receive reviews
- `listings 1—* favorites` — Listings can be favorited
- `listings 1—* reports` — Listings can be reported for fraud
- `tours 1—* reviews` — Completed tours can generate reviews
- `amenities 1—* listing_amenities`
- `treatments_services 1—* listing_services`
- `equipment 1—* listing_equipment`
- `languages 1—* listing_languages`
- `insurance_options 1—* listing_insurance_options`

---

# Example Tour Booking Flow

Example tour booking process for senior housing:

```text
1. Family searches for senior housing in Los Angeles
2. Filters by: Care Level (Assisted Living), Monthly Budget ($3000-5000), Amenities, Certifications, Insurance Accepted, Availability
3. Views listing details with images, amenities, and care types
4. Compares multiple listings side-by-side
5. Saves preferred listings to favorites
6. Selects a listing and books a tour (in-person)
7. Provider receives tour request and approves
8. System sends confirmation to family
9. Family visits facility for tour
10. After visit, family leaves 4-star review
11. Provider responds to review
12. Family decides on placement (outside system scope)
```

The system:

1. Tracks the search and filter criteria (location, care level, monthly budget, amenities, certifications, insurance accepted, availability, ratings)
2. Stores favorites for later comparison
3. Manages tour booking workflow (request → approval → confirmation)
4. Tracks tour bookings and status
5. Generates reviews after tours
6. Updates listing statistics and ratings
7. Supports map-based location search and distance filtering

---

# End of Document
